scope.summary: Implement the register_trim_as_library_item MCP tool and kernel handler that marks an existing TRIM- row as is_library_item=TRUE with an operator-supplied display name, making it resolvable by name or category in subsequent GarmentSpec authoring sessions across the same workspace
acceptance_criteria: A TRIM- row can be promoted to is_library_item=TRUE via the register handler; the display_name column (TEXT) is set at registration time
Library-registered trims are listed in a GET /tailor/trim-library endpoint scoped to workspace_id, sorted by trim_category and display_name
A model or operator can reference a registered accessory by display_name in a GarmentSpec TrimPlacementRef without knowing the TRIM- id
TailorTrimImported event (already required by TR-10.1) is enriched with is_library_item=true in the payload when a trim is registered as a library item
A migration adds display_name TEXT column to tailor_trims if not already present
scope.risk_if_missed: Custom hardware assets imported by the operator (brand-specific buckles, proprietary fasteners) cannot be saved as reusable library items; every garment that needs the same custom trim must re-import it, breaking the operator workflow and making the trim import system (proposed gap MT above) a one-shot dead end rather than a productive asset pipeline

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
